notes.anchor: the fifth before the Nones of May
notes.note: Roman dates were counted backwards (inclusively) from three fixed points each month: the Kalends (1st), Nones (5th or 7th), and Ides (13th or 15th). The fifth before the Nones of May is 3 May 44 BC.

A freedman carrying Atticus's letter; "Demetrius" is too common a freedman's name to bind with confidence to any of the several Demetrii in the corpus, so the surface is left unresolved (flag-not-resolve).

Buthrotum (modern Butrint, Epirus) was the seat of Atticus's estates; its townsmen faced confiscation of land to settle Caesar's veterans, and Cicero spent much of 44 BC lobbying Antony to spare them. The "Buthrotian business" is this recurring effort.
L. Iulius Caesar, consul 64 BC and maternal uncle of Mark Antony; a moderate consular whose pessimism about Antony's intentions Cicero here cites with alarm as he found him gravely ill at Naples.
The Kalends (1st) of June 44 BC, the date set for the Senate meeting at which Antony's land and colony measures (including the Buthrotum case) were to be decided.
Quintus Cicero the younger, Cicero's nephew, who in 44 BC attached himself to Antony as a partisan; Cicero repeatedly calls him a lost cause. Distinct from his father, Cicero's brother Quintus.

Aquilia, the dowered second wife taken (or proposed) by Cicero's brother Quintus; young Quintus's refusal to tolerate her is the surface grievance of the bitter letter, with the dowry the underlying friction.
Renders the Greek tag melesei ("it will be a care to me / it will be seen to"), one of Cicero's habitual code-switches into Greek to round off a vexed subject with affected lightness.

Renders the Greek anekdoton ("not given out," unpublished); Cicero means a private, still-unpolished political work he is withholding, fearing it is now more dangerous to attack the Caesarian party with Caesar dead than it was while he lived.
L. Tullius Montanus, a young companion of Cicero's son at Athens for whose debts Cicero had stood surety; the tangled "Montanus affair" recurs across these letters.
